metadata.content:
# Wi-Fi Configuration with Captive Portal

## Overview
This template implements a Wi-Fi configuration system with captive portal functionality for easy network setup. It provides a web-based interface for scanning, selecting, and connecting to Wi-Fi networks.

## Features
- **Captive Portal**: Automatic configuration page when no Wi-Fi is configured
- **Network Scanning**: Real-time Wi-Fi network discovery
- **Persistent Storage**: Configuration saved to EEPROM
- **Status Indication**: LED feedback for connection status
- **Reset Function**: Hardware button to reset settings
- **mDNS Support**: Local network discovery
- **AP Fallback**: Access point mode when connection fails
- **Mobile Responsive**: Web interface works on all devices

## Configuration Options
- **LED Pin**: Status LED pin (default: 2)
- **Button Pin**: Reset button pin (default: 0)
- **Portal Timeout**: Captive portal timeout in minutes (default: 10)
- **Connection Timeout**: Wi-Fi connection timeout in seconds (default: 30)
- **Max Retries**: Maximum connection retry attempts (default: 5)
- **Device Name**: Device name for identification (default: "ATHENA-WiFi-Config")
- **Default SSID**: Access point SSID (default: "ATHENA-Setup")
- **Enable AP Fallback**: Enable AP mode on connection failure (default: true)
- **Enable mDNS**: Enable mDNS discovery (default: true)

## Web Interface Features
- **Network Scanner**: Discover available Wi-Fi networks
- **Signal Strength**: Display RSSI for each network
- **Security Indication**: Show encryption type
- **Quick Connect**: Click to select networks
- **Status Feedback**: Real-time connection status
- **Settings Reset**: Clear saved configuration

## LED Status Patterns
- **Solid ON**: Connected to Wi-Fi
- **Slow Blink**: Connecting or portal active
- **Fast Blink**: Connection error
- **OFF**: Disconnected

## Button Functions
- **Short Press**: No action
- **Long Press (3+ seconds)**: Reset Wi-Fi settings

## Supported Boards
- ESP32 DevKit v1
- ESP8266 NodeMCU v2

## Dependencies
- WiFi library (built-in)
- WebServer library (built-in)
- DNSServer library (built-in)
- EEPROM library (built-in)
- ArduinoJson v6.21.3

## Setup Instructions
1. Connect LED to pin {{.ledPin}} with 220Ω resistor
2. Connect push button to pin {{.buttonPin}} with pull-up resistor
3. Power on the device
4. Connect to "{{.defaultSSID}}" Wi-Fi network
5. Open browser and navigate to configuration page
6. Select network and enter password
7. Device will automatically connect and restart

## Advanced Configuration
- **Custom AP Settings**: Modify default SSID and password
- **Timeout Adjustments**: Configure portal and connection timeouts
- **Retry Logic**: Adjust maximum retry attempts
- **mDNS Integration**: Enable local network discovery

## Troubleshooting
- **Portal Not Accessible**: Check device power and AP settings
- **Connection Fails**: Verify SSID/password and network availability
- **LED Not Working**: Check LED connections and pin assignments
- **Button Not Responding**: Verify button wiring and pull-up resistor
- **Settings Not Saving**: Check EEPROM functionality

## Security Considerations
- **Default Password**: Change default AP password
- **Network Security**: Use WPA2/WPA3 networks
- **Data Protection**: Credentials stored in EEPROM
- **Access Control**: Limit portal access time

## Integration Options
- **IoT Platforms**: Compatible with Home Assistant, OpenHAB
- **Cloud Services**: Works with AWS IoT, Azure IoT
- **Mobile Apps**: Custom app development possible
- **API Integration**: RESTful configuration API

## Performance Specifications
- **Network Scan Time**: <5 seconds for 10 networks
- **Connection Time**: <30 seconds typical
- **Memory Usage**: ~40KB for web server
- **Power Consumption**: ~80mA (Wi-Fi active)
- **Range**: Standard Wi-Fi range (~50m indoor)

## Use Cases
- **IoT Device Setup**: Easy configuration for smart devices
- **Industrial Sensors**: Network configuration for monitoring
- **Home Automation**: Smart home device provisioning
- **Retrofits**: Adding Wi-Fi to existing devices
- **Educational Projects**: Learning Wi-Fi programming
